lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Arr, Matey!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm in search of treasure.

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Arr, treasure you be after eh? Well I might be able to tell you where to find some... For a price...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What sort of price?

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Well for example if you can get me a bottle of rum... Not just any rum mind...

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: I'd like some rum made on Karamja Island. There's no rum like Karamja Rum!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would some Braindeath 'Rum' do?

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Gadzooks, that swill! Not a chance!

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: It's Karamja Rum or no deal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I will bring you some rum.

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Yer a saint, although it'll take a miracle to get it off Karamja.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: The Customs office has been clampin' down on the export of spirits. You seem like a resourceful young [lad/lass], I'm sure ye'll be able to find a way to slip the stuff past them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'll give it a shot.

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Arr, that's the spirit!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now.

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Fair enough. I'll still be here and thirsty whenever you feel like helpin' out.

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Arr, Matey!

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Have ye brought some rum for yer ol' mate Frank?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Not surprising, tis no easy task to get it off Karamja.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: The Customs office has been clampin' down on the export of spirits. You seem like a resourceful young lad, I'm sure ye'll be able to find a way to slip the stuff past them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'll give it another shot.

lines.speaker: Luthas
lines.text: Hello I'm Luthas, I run the banana plantation here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you offer me employment on your plantation?

lines.speaker: Luthas
lines.text: Yes, I can sort something out. There's a crate ready to be loaded onto the ship.

lines.speaker: Luthas
lines.text: You wouldn't believe the demand for bananas from Wydin's shop over in Port Sarim. I think this is the third crate I've shipped him this month..

lines.speaker: Luthas
lines.text: If you could fill it up with bananas, I'll pay you 30 gold.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That customs officer is annoying isn't she?

lines.speaker: Luthas
lines.text: Well I know her pretty well. She doesn't cause me any trouble any more.

lines.speaker: Luthas
lines.text: She doesn't even search my export crates any more. She knows they only contain bananas.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? How interesting. Whereabouts do you send those to?

lines.speaker: Luthas
lines.text: There is a little shop over in Port Sarim that buys them up by the crate. I believe it is run by a man called Wydin.

sections.title: Attempting to hide Karamjan rum (banana) in the crate
Attempting to hide Karamjan rum (sliced banana) in the crate
Talking to Luthas after filling up the crate

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've filled a crate with bananas.

lines.speaker: Luthas
lines.text: Well done, here's your payment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will you pay me for another crate full?

lines.speaker: Luthas
lines.text: Yes certainly.

lines.speaker: Luthas
lines.text: If you go outside you should see the old crate has been loaded on to the ship, and there is another empty crate in its place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you, I'll be on my way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where are these bananas going to be delivered to?

lines.speaker: Luthas
lines.text: I sell them to Wydin who runs a grocery store in Port Sarim.

lines.speaker: Customs officer
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I journey on this ship?

lines.speaker: Customs officer
lines.text: You need to be searched before you can board.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Customs officer
lines.text: Because Asgarnia has banned the import of intoxicating spirits.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Search away, I have nothing to hide.

lines.speaker: Customs officer
lines.text: Aha, trying to smuggle rum are we?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Umm... it's for personal use?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is not the Karamja rum you are looking for.

lines.speaker: Customs officer
lines.text: This is not the Karamja rum we are looking for.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that was easy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Drat...

lines.speaker: Customs officer
lines.text: Well you've got some odd stuff, but it's all legal. Now you need to pay a boarding charge of 30 coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear, I don't seem to have enough money.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I'll not bother then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're not putting your hands on my things!

lines.speaker: Customs officer
lines.text: You're not getting on this ship then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't need to look through my things.

lines.speaker: Customs officer
lines.text: Oh, I think I do. If you don't want to let me search your possessions, then you're not coming aboard this ship.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does Karamja have any unusual customs then?

lines.speaker: Customs officer
lines.text: I'm not that sort of customs officer.

sections.title: Attempting to smuggle out the rum

lines.speaker: Imp
lines.text: Don't you know? You can get into trouble for giving alcohol to minors, and I'm pretty small, it has to be said.

lines.speaker: Wydin
lines.text: Welcome to my food store! Would you like to buy anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I get a job here?

lines.speaker: Wydin
lines.text: Well, you're keen, I'll give you that. Okay, I'll give you a go. Have you got your own white apron?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have one right here.

lines.speaker: Wydin
lines.text: Wow - you are well prepared! You're hired. Go through to the back and tidy up for me, please.

lines.speaker: Wydin
lines.text: You need to put your white apron on first though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I haven't.

lines.speaker: Wydin
lines.text: Well, you can't work here unless you have a white apron. Health and safety regulations, you understand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get one of those?

lines.speaker: Wydin
lines.text: Well, I get all of mine over at the clothing shop in Varrock. They sell them cheap there.

lines.speaker: Wydin
lines.text: Oh, and I'm sure that I've seen a spare one over in Gerrant's fish store somewhere. It's the little place just north of here.

sections.title: Trying to open the door in the Food Store before getting the job

lines.speaker: Wydin
lines.text: Hey, you can't go in there. Only employees of the grocery store can go in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I didn't realise.

sections.title: Trying to open the door after getting the job while not wearing a white apron

lines.speaker: Wydin
lines.text: Can you put your white apron on before going in there, please?

sections.title: Talking to Wydin after getting the job

lines.speaker: Wydin
lines.text: Is it nice and tidy round the back now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, can I work out front now?

lines.speaker: Wydin
lines.text: No, I'm the one who works here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, are you going to pay me yet?

lines.speaker: Wydin
lines.text: Umm... No, not yet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, it's a complete mess.

lines.speaker: Wydin
lines.text: Ah well, it'll give you something to do, won't it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I buy something please?

lines.speaker: Wydin
lines.text: Yes, of course.

sections.title: Taking the apron in Gerrant's Fishy Business
Search crate in the Food Store

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Arr, Matey!

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Have ye brought some rum for yer ol' mate Frank?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I've got some.

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Arr - this here rum's got a banana stuck in it!

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Arr, Matey!

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Have ye brought some rum for yer ol' mate Frank?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I've got some.

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Arr - I don't likes banana in me rum!

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Arr, Matey!

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Have ye brought some rum for yer ol' mate Frank?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Not surprising, tis no easy task to get it off Karamja.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: The Customs office has been clampin' down on the export of spirits. You seem like a resourceful young [lad/lass], I'm sure ye'll be able to find a way to slip the stuff past them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'll give it another shot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I've got some.

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Now a deal's a deal, I'll tell ye about the treasure. I used to serve under a pirate captain called One-Eyed Hector.

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Hector were very successful and became very rich. But about a year ago we were boarded by the Customs and Excise Agents.

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Hector were killed along with many of the crew, I were one of the few to escape and I escaped with this.

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: This be Hector's key. I believe it opens his chest in his old room in the Blue Moon Inn in Varrock.

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: With any luck his treasure will be in there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok thanks, I'll go and get it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why didn't you ever get it?

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: I'm not allowed in the Blue Moon Inn. Apparently I'm a drunken trouble maker.

sections.title: Using the rum on Redbeard Frank

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Frank, I have some Karamja Rum.

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Now a deal's a deal, I'll tell ye about the treasure. I used to serve under a pirate captain called One-Eyed Hector.

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Arr, Matey!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seem to have lost my chest key...

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: Arr, silly you. Fortunately I took the precaution to have another one made.

sections.title: Using the key on the Chest

lines.speaker: Gardener
lines.text: First moles, now this! Take this, vandal!

sections.title: Digging in Falador Park while being attacked by the Gardener
Digging in Falador Park a second time
Using the chest on Redbeard Frank after the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the treasure, would you like a share?

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: No [lad/lass], you got it fair and square.

lines.speaker: Redbeard Frank
lines.text: You enjoy it. It's what Hector would have wanted.
